name: Crispy Quail Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Chinese Chile Paste with Garlic
1/4 cup Dry White Wine or Water
1 cup Julienned Red Bell Pepper - seeded, stemmed
1/2 cup Scallions - cut on the bias
recipeInstructions:
For the Quail Salad:
1. In a blender, combine hoisin sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, chile paste, garlic, ginger, and wine. Pulse to make a smooth mixture. Whisk together and add quail; toss to coat evenly. Refrigerate marinated quail for at least 2 hours.
2. Heat oil in a deep pan to 350°F (use a thermometer). Combine flour, cayenne, 5 spice powder, and salt on a plate.
3. Remove quail from marinade and wipe off excess; lightly coat with the flour mixture.
4. Deep fry quail in batches until golden brown and crispy (6-8 minutes). Check leg joint to ensure cooked through. Keep quail warm in a low oven and return oil to 350°F before frying more pieces.
5. Toss peppers, jicama, scallions, and carrots with about 1/3 cup of the vinaigrette. Arrange attractively on plates. Top each plate with a warm fried quail and garnish with cilantro sprigs. Serve immediately.
For the Honey-Lemon Vinaigrette:
1. In a mixing bowl, sauté shallots in olive oil until soft but not brown.
2. Whisk in lemon juice, honey (or to taste), salt, and freshly ground black pepper.
3. Refrigerate vinaigrette up to 5 days.
Yield: 6 servings
